title: Cardiology Consultation - Heart Failure Management
author: Dr. James Wilson

sections.reasonForConsultation: Heart failure management, optimization of guideline-directed medical therapy
sections.historyOfPresentIllness:
Mr. Morrison is a 72-year-old gentleman with long-standing heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, last documented EF 30-35% in August 2023, who is referred for outpatient cardiology follow-up and optimization of heart failure therapy.

Patient reports overall stability since his last hospitalization in September 2023 for upper GI hemorrhage, which was complicated by hemorrhagic shock requiring ICU admission and 4 units of packed red blood cells. EGD revealed a large gastric ulcer with visible vessel, treated with epinephrine injection, bipolar cautery, and hemoclip. H. pylori was positive and has been treated. Notably, warfarin was permanently discontinued during that admission after multidisciplinary discussion (GI, Cardiology, PCP) given the severity of the bleed and high rebleed risk. He is now on aspirin 81 mg daily only for stroke risk reduction, with an accepted increase in thromboembolic risk (CHA2DS2-VASc score 5).

Prior to the GI bleed, his last CHF hospitalization was August 2022 for acute decompensated heart failure triggered by diuretic non-compliance. Since discharge from the September GI bleed admission, he has been doing well from a cardiac standpoint. He denies significant dyspnea at rest, though he does experience exertional dyspnea after walking 1-2 blocks. He has been compliant with all medications and has not missed any doses of his diuretic. He weighs himself daily and has maintained his weight at approximately 97-98 kg. He follows a low-sodium diet and limits his fluid intake to 1.5 liters daily.

He does report intermittent bilateral lower extremity edema, typically worse at the end of the day, which improves overnight and with leg elevation. He denies orthopnea or paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea. He has occasional palpitations consistent with his known atrial fibrillation but denies sustained palpitations, lightheadedness, or syncope.

He was transitioned from losartan to sacubitril/valsartan (Entresto) in February 2020 and has been on the target dose of 97/103 mg BID since July 2020. He tolerated this well until the August 2022 CHF admission, when it was temporarily reduced, but has been back on target dose since September 2022.

His last echocardiogram from August 2023 showed EF 30-35% with regional wall motion abnormalities (inferior and inferolateral akinesis), moderate mitral regurgitation, and severely dilated left atrium.
currentMedications: Aspirin 81 mg PO daily (for A-fib stroke prevention; warfarin discontinued 9/2023 post-GI bleed)
Insulin Glargine (Lantus) 24 units subcutaneous at bedtime
Insulin Lispro (Humalog) sliding scale with meals
Gabapentin 300 mg PO three times daily
Pantoprazole 40 mg PO daily (indefinite, post-GI bleed)

physicalExamination.general: Well-appearing male in no acute distress
physicalExamination.neck: JVP approximately 8 cm H2O at 45 degrees, no carotid bruits
physicalExamination.cardiovascular: Irregularly irregular rhythm, normal S1, physiologically split S2, soft S3 gallop, 2/6 holosystolic murmur at apex radiating to axilla (mitral regurgitation), no rubs
physicalExamination.lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally, no wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi
physicalExamination.abdomen: Soft, non-tender, no hepatomegaly or ascites
physicalExamination.extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema, warm, good peripheral pulses

recentEcho.findings: EF 30-35%, global hypokinesis with inferior and inferolateral akinesis, moderate MR (central jet), severely dilated LA (volume index 52 mL/m2), mild TR with RVSP 42 mmHg

diagnostics.recentEkg: Atrial fibrillation with controlled ventricular response at 68 bpm, left axis deviation, old inferior Q waves, non-specific ST-T changes

sections.assessment:
72-year-old male with ischemic cardiomyopathy (HFrEF, EF 30-35%), currently NYHA Class II. He is on guideline-directed medical therapy including beta-blocker (carvedilol), ARNI (Entresto at target dose), aldosterone antagonist (spironolactone), and loop diuretic (furosemide). He is euvolemic to mildly volume overloaded today.

Significant recent events:
- GI hemorrhage (September 2023) resulting in permanent warfarin discontinuation. Now on aspirin 81 mg only for A-fib stroke prevention. CHA2DS2-VASc score of 5 confers annual stroke risk of approximately 6.7%. This was a carefully considered multidisciplinary decision that I participated in and continue to support given the severity of the index bleed.

His HF therapy is currently submaximal. Specifically:
1. Carvedilol dose is 12.5 mg BID (target dose 25 mg BID) — has been at this dose since prior to the GI bleed; was not uptitrated during the acute recovery period
2. Not on SGLT2 inhibitor — empagliflozin would provide mortality benefit (EMPEROR-Reduced) and has additional renal protective benefits given his CKD 3b
3. ARNI (Entresto) is at target dose — this is appropriate
4. Spironolactone at 25 mg daily — appropriate, monitoring potassium

items: Increase carvedilol to 18.75 mg BID in 2 weeks if BP and HR tolerate — patient has been at 12.5 mg BID for over a year; uptitration was deferred during GI bleed recovery but should proceed now
Continue Entresto 97/103 mg BID — already at target dose, well tolerated
Add empagliflozin 10 mg daily (SGLT2i has mortality benefit in HFrEF per EMPEROR-Reduced, additional CKD benefit per EMPA-KIDNEY, and glycemic benefit given his DM)
Continue current diuretic regimen - patient is near euvolemic; weight stable at 97-98 kg
Patient has EF ≤35% on maximally tolerated GDMT for >3 months. He meets criteria for primary prevention ICD.
Discussed ICD placement with patient. He is interested but would like to discuss with family. Will schedule separate visit to discuss device therapy in detail.
Continue aspirin and statin for secondary prevention
Cardiac rehabilitation referral - would benefit from supervised exercise program
Recheck BMP and BNP in 4 weeks after medication changes
Daily weights, call if gain >3 lbs in 1 day or >5 lbs in 1 week

sections.patientEducation: Discussed heart failure management including importance of medication compliance, daily weights, sodium restriction, and symptom monitoring. Discussed SGLT2 inhibitor benefits and side effects (UTI risk, genital yeast infections, euglycemic DKA). Discussed ICD for primary prevention of sudden cardiac death. Patient and wife present, both verbalized understanding.
sections.plan:
1. Increase carvedilol to 18.75 mg BID in 2 weeks (will call patient to confirm tolerating)
2. Recommend empagliflozin 10 mg daily — discussed with patient, he is interested but wants to 'think about it' before adding another medication. Will revisit at follow-up.
3. Continue Entresto 97/103 mg BID (at target)
4. Refer to cardiac rehab
5. Follow-up in 4-6 weeks
6. Schedule separate visit to discuss ICD with patient and family
7. Note: Anticoagulation remains permanently held per multidisciplinary decision (9/2023). Will continue to monitor for signs/symptoms of stroke at each visit.

attestation:
I personally evaluated this patient, reviewed the available data, and formulated the assessment and recommendations. I have discussed the findings and plan with the patient and his wife.

Electronically signed by: James Wilson, MD, FACC
Date/Time: 11/15/2023 2:00 PM
